what_it_does: Records a correction and expansion note (PART 3) to a prior planning item, reframing who executes total coverage and what existing systems must converge to build the directing interface.
observations: Self-identified as feedback-driven corrections after Tim's review
Argues total coverage must be run by the Company's concurrent machinery (dragnet, local models, multiple Claude Codes), not the current session
Identifies three upfront convergence tasks: unify split channel systems, make the board two-way, merge three disconnected comment/annotation stores
Lists decision cards and chat as additional input modes alongside annotation
Describes a thin coherent bootstrap slice to break the fabric/interface/Tim dependency
Emphasizes recognition over interrogation and a presentation-preference-learning seam
standouts: Reversal of §2.9: coverage executor is company machinery, not the current session
Convergence of existing fragmented systems is the upfront work, not a fresh UI overlay
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Markdown body with numbered sections and subheadings
References other board items via board:// URIs
Uses bold and bullet lists for emphasis
concerns: Title contains HTML entity & instead of a plain ampersand
Assumes reader knows prior parts §2.6–2.9 without restating them
Some key terms (dragnet, local concurrent models, multiple Claude Codes, RHM) are not defined in this file
Heavy capitalization and rhetorical emphasis may obscure factual content
notes: A course-correction planning note that reframes coverage as company-fabric work and sizes the upfront convergence needed for Tim's directing interface.
questions: What exactly is the dragnet and how is it invoked?
What is the programmatic directory-diff completeness gate?
What are the precise differences between the two channel systems and their member-identity models?
What is the current ledger schema being filled?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is corrections and expansion after Tim's feedback; the file does exactly that, restating and reframing prior sections.
apparent_themes: Separation of coordinator and laborer roles
intention_signals: Redirect the project away from using the current Claude Code session as the coverage laborer and toward mobilizing existing company-scale concurrent machinery, while honestly sizing the upfront convergence work needed to give Tim a usable directing surface.
novelty: Treats the interface build as primarily a merge of already-existing but disconnected channel, board, and annotation systems rather than a new layer over the ledger.
contribution: The corrected architectural stance that coverage is a company-fabric responsibility and the inventory of fragmented systems that must converge before a coherent directing interface exists.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note titled PART 3 — CORRECTIONS & EXPANSION (after Tim's feedback) that supersedes §2.9 and expands §2.6–2.7, arguing total coverage must be executed by the Company's concurrent machinery and that the interface should be built by converging existing split channel, board, and comment/annotation systems.
intention_for_embedding: To correct the prior plan by removing the current session as the coverage executor and scoping the upfront convergence work required for Tim's recognition-based directing interface.
